one_time_description: Indigenous Services Canada forgiveness of Treaty Loan in prior year (2020)
economic_notes: Nation has interests in Homalco Holdings Limited Partnership, Homalco Wildlife Tours Inc., Port Neville Logging Company, and Homalco Resources Management Limited Partnership. Earnings from GBEs of $643,933 in 2021. Forensic audit recovery of $328,331 still under negotiation with ISC.
notes: Audit is qualified for three reasons: (1) no approved budget prepared for the year, (2) deferred revenue not recognized at March 31, 2020 affecting opening accumulated surplus, (3) GBE financials were unaudited and some related party information was unavailable. Prior year (2020) included a one-time $8,284,361 ISC forgiveness of Treaty Loan. Four GBEs accounted for by modified equity method. Three fully consolidated subsidiaries: Bute Inlet Development Corporation, Homalco Holdings Inc., and Homalco Trust. Contingent liability of $328,331 for ISC forensic audit recoveries still under negotiation. Community Services segment expenses include only $76,790. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $3,644,004 + FNHA $1,035,588 + Province of BC $426,359 + DFO $254,081 + CMHC $78,404 + BC Treaty Commission $325,000 + First Peoples Cultural Council $157,917 = $5,921,353; however FNHA and First Peoples Cultural Council may be non-government, so conservative estimate used sum of ISC + Province of BC + DFO + CMHC + BC Treaty Commission = $4,727,844 plus FNHA $1,035,588 = $5,763,432 rounded to $5,763,440.
